name: Night Companion
short_description: A temporary partnership between explorers to take turns on watch during the night.

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as a slang term for temporary overnight partnerships; the protagonist forms one with Hans.

information.label: Definition
information.notes: Slang for a temporary partnership between explorers to take turns on watch during the night. Best with three members, but two also work.

name: Hans
short_description: Human explorer who becomes Bjorn's Night Companion. Attempted to kill Bjorn to sell his heart, but was killed by Bjorn in self-defense.

chapter_mentions.context: Proposes a Night Companion partnership to the protagonist, showing experience and trust.
Attempts to kill Bjorn while he sleeps but fails; Bjorn kills him in self-defense, revealing his motive was greed for Bjorn's heart.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Kind, gentle face.

information.label: Equipment
information.notes: Wields a Mace coated in Goblin blood; carries a Pocket Watch with numbers 0-23.

information.label: Behavior
information.notes: Experienced; takes initiative in forming Night Companion arrangements; trusts easily but is cautious in lending valuable items.

information.label: Motivation
information.notes: Wanted to kill Bjorn to sell his heart as an ingredient for a newly developed magic.

information.label: Fate
information.notes: Killed by Bjorn with a shield blow to the face.

name: Yandel
short_description: Bjorn's father. Mentioned only as a patronymic in Bjorn's self-introduction ('son of Yandel').

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as Bjorn's father through his patronymic.

name: Smash
short_description: A skill used by Bjorn to attack enemies with his shield or hammer. It has decent attack power and consumes no MP.

chapter_mentions.context: Used effectively with the newly equipped two-handed hammer to defeat goblins.

information.label: Usage
information.notes: Bjorn uses Smash with both shield and hammer; with a proper weapon its power is on another level.

name: First Floor
short_description: The lowest floor of the Labyrinth. It contains the Goblin District and is where most explorers begin their adventure.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn explores the First Floor, noting its size and the scarcity of Barbarians.

information.label: General
information.notes: Very few Barbarians remain on the First Floor for long; most adventurers encountered are human.

name: Pocket Watch
short_description: A time-telling device, lent by the Old Man Hans and now used by Bjorn.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn uses it to check the time.

name: Richo Leaf
short_description: A herb used for healing wounds. Identified by the Fairy.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn gives the Richo Leaf to the wounded Fairy to treat her injury.

information.label: Healing Property
information.notes: Used by applying to wounds; the Fairy identifies it and Bjorn tosses it to her.

name: Rafdonia
short_description: A deity or blessing mentioned by an explorer; 'blessing of Rafdonia'.

chapter_mentions.context: An explorer wishes the blessing of Rafdonia upon Bjorn's journey.

name: Serdin
short_description: A member of the Crystal Alliance who was tracking Bjorn and Erwen. Killed by Erwen's arrow.

chapter_mentions.context: Killed by Erwen to prevent him from alerting allies.

information.label: Death
information.notes: Shot through the forehead by Erwen's arrow while trying to communicate via Message Stone.

name: Goblin District
short_description: A region on the First Floor of the Labyrinth where goblins are commonly found. Also mentioned as an assembly point for the Crystal Alliance.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn has been moving through the Goblin District since entering the Labyrinth.

name: Message Stone
short_description: A magical tool that allows communication within about 300 meters when resonated with another stone. Used by the Crystal Alliance to coordinate pursuit. Bjorn obtained one from the corpse of Serdin.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Crystal Alliance to coordinate pursuit; Bjorn obtains one from the corpse of Serdin.

information.label: Range
information.notes: Communication only possible between stones that have been resonated together beforehand.

name: Ghoul
short_description: A ninth-rank undead monster found on the First Floor, specifically in the western region. It is stronger than Goblins, with rotten skin, empty eye sockets, and sharp claws. It moves on all fours and only attacks when approached within a certain range.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn encounters Ghouls near the boundary of the Goblin District.

information.label: Behavior
information.notes: Remains stationary until a character gets within a certain range, then charges. Defeated easily by Bjorn's Hammer Smash.

name: Blade Wolves
short_description: A type of monster mentioned by explorers on the First Floor as a hunting target.

chapter_mentions.context: Mentioned by other explorers as a potential hunting target.

name: Second Floor
short_description: The second floor of the Labyrinth, accessed via portals from the First Floor. It is a dark forest with a high ceiling that appears as a starry sky, known as the Goblin Forest at its entrance. Monsters appear in larger groups (ten or more) and include more dangerous varieties like Mutants and higher-rank creatures. The forest is filled with numerous traps and extremely tough trees that cannot be knocked down.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn decides to head to the Second Floor with Erwen to escape pursuit and increase earnings.
Bjorn and Erwen arrive on the Second Floor, a dark forest with a starry ceiling and numerous traps, requiring careful navigation.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: A dark forest with a high ceiling dotted with star-like lights providing dim visibility (comparable to an alleyway without streetlights). The 'sky' is actually a high ceiling and does not brighten with time. Trees are incredibly tough, resisting hammer strikes. The ground is littered with hidden traps.

name: Goblin Forest
short_description: The specific area of the Second Floor entered by Bjorn and Erwen after activating the First Floor portal. A dark forest with many traps and goblin groups. Trees are extremely tough and cannot be knocked down. The environment is dimly lit by star-like lights on a high ceiling.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Erwen enter the Goblin Forest on the Second Floor, immediately noticing its darkness and the prevalence of traps.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: A dark forest with a high ceiling dotted with star-like lights providing dim visibility (comparable to an alleyway without streetlights). The 'sky' is actually a high ceiling and does not brighten with time. Trees are incredibly tough, resisting hammer strikes. The ground is littered with hidden traps.

name: Goblin Archer's Essence
short_description: A rare essence dropped by a Goblin Archer. Upon absorption, it grants stat increases (Agility +2, Flexibility +4, Vision +6, Scent +2, Awareness +2, Concealment +6, Accuracy +8, Poison Resistance +4, Tenacity +7), a passive skill (Poison Arrow: poison damage with bow-type weapons), and an active skill (Thief's Step: stealth state). Erwen absorbed it in this chapter, gaining the ability to become partially invisible.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn discovers the Essence after defeating a Goblin Archer, and offers it to Erwen as part of a deal.
Erwen absorbed it, gaining the stealth ability (Thief's Step) and other goblin archer traits.

information.label: Effect

attributes.effect: Grants a portion of the Goblin Archer's abilities when absorbed

information.notes: Essences are the skill system of Dungeon & Stone; absorbing one grants some of the monster's abilities. The exact ability gained is not specified beyond 'a portion of the abilities'.

information.label: Skills Granted
information.notes: Poison Arrow adds poison damage to bow attacks; Thief's Step grants a stealth state that is more effective when standing still.

name: Persi
short_description: Crossbowman in the group of four explorers; killed by Erwen's arrow.

chapter_mentions.context: Killed by Erwen during the ambush; was part of the group that attacked Bjorn and Erwen.

name: Illis
short_description: Samurai in the group of four explorers; killed by Bjorn biting his neck.

chapter_mentions.context: Killed by Bjorn in combat; was a skilled swordsman but overwhelmed.

name: Black Whale Tavern

aliases.name: Paidenfuss
aliases.context: Current name of the tavern after ownership changed.

short_description: Tavern in Rafdonia where Bjorn and Erwen agreed to meet. It closed over ten years ago and is now operating under the name Paidenfuss, after the original owner's son got into gambling and the owner changed.

chapter_mentions.context: Agreed upon as the meeting place for Bjorn and Erwen after returning to Rafdonia.
Bjorn learns from a local that the Black Whale Tavern closed over ten years ago and is now operating under the name Paidenfuss.

name: Labyrinth
short_description: The dungeon where explorers navigate and fight monsters. It has multiple floors, with the Sixth Floor mentioned as a goal. Time in the Labyrinth passes differently than in the city. The first year after entering has reduced taxes and fee exemptions, making it the optimal period for rapid growth. The floors are accessed via portals.

chapter_mentions.context: Protagonist explores the Labyrinth, finding it well-lit and less threatening in open areas, yet still dangerous.
Bjorn sets reaching the Sixth Floor as his intermediate goal for long-term financial security.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Caves with walls and ceiling embedded with crystals that emit light, making the interiors well-lit. Complex, ant-hill-like structure.

information.label: Features
information.notes: Contains ponds as water sources; Goblins set crude traps in open areas.

name: Sixth Floor
short_description: A floor of the Labyrinth; Bjorn calculates that to live comfortably until retirement, he needs to reach at least the Sixth Floor. It is a concrete intermediate goal for financial security.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn calculates the financial requirements for retirement and sets reaching the Sixth Floor as his intermediate goal.

name: Central Plaza
short_description: A large plaza in Rafdonia serving as a crossroads. It is the site of public executions of tax evaders, where prisoners are guillotined and spectators sometimes dip bread in the blood.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Erwen pass through the Central Plaza and witness the execution of tax evaders.

name: Dungeon & Stone
short_description: The game Bjorn played in his original world; its name is a forbidden word that could expose him as an Evil Spirit. It has three main stats (Physical, Mental, Supernatural Ability) and thousands of sub-stats. Sub-stats like Flexibility increase evasion and critical hit; Vision increases ranged range; Smell affects related abilities; Obsession strengthens desires. The game's mechanics have real-world effects beyond the original game.

chapter_mentions.context: Referred to as a peculiar game; the protagonist notes its hardcore nature and the prevalence of companion NPCs.
Bjorn reflects on sub-stats like Obsession and Flexibility, noting their real-world effects beyond the game.
The name is a forbidden word that could expose Bjorn as an Evil Spirit.

information.label: Game Mechanics
information.notes: Has a satiety system that is only active inside the Labyrinth; skills can be improvised by players.

information.label: Forbidden Word
information.notes: Uttering the name 'Dungeon & Stone' could expose Bjorn as an Evil Spirit, as it is a forbidden word according to the book.

name: First King Ravigion III

aliases.name: First King
aliases.context: Referred to as the First King in the book title.

short_description: The Immortal King whose death 150 years ago marked the timeline shift; mentioned in the book 'On the Death of the King'.

chapter_mentions.context: His death 150 years ago is revealed in the book 'On the Death of the King', proving that the current timeline is 150 years after the game's start.

name: On the Death of the King
short_description: A book detailing the death of the First King Ravigion III, which occurred 150 years ago, proving the timeline shift.

chapter_mentions.context: Revealed that the king died 150 years ago, confirming the timeline is 150 years after the game's start.

name: Observation Records of Dimensional Instability
short_description: A book containing statistics about explorers falling into the Outermost Area and speculation that portal closing time may be related to instability.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn reads it and realizes that falling into the Outermost Area is rare; author speculates about portal timing.

information.label: Author's Speculation
information.notes: The author speculates that entering the portal the moment it closes may be related to the instability phenomenon.

name: Compendium of Ninth-Rank Essences
short_description: An encyclopedia listing abilities of Essences from Ninth Rank monsters, using qualitative tiers (Low, Mid) instead of exact numbers.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn uses it to understand stat tiers; notes that High rank stats correspond to 21+ in a stat.

information.label: Stat Tier System
information.notes: Stats are categorized as Low, Mid, or High. A stat of 21 or higher is marked as 'Mid'. Information on Rank 8 and above must be purchased from the Explorer's Guild.

name: Outermost Area
short_description: A dangerous region in the Labyrinth where explorers rarely fall; mentioned in the observation records.

chapter_mentions.context: Mentioned in the observation records as a rare occurrence; Bjorn experienced it.

name: Raging Horn Bull Inn
short_description: An inn in Rafdonia where Ainar and other Barbarians stay. It is cheap (300 Stone per day) because rooms are shared by 10 people with only 5 beds, so they sleep in shifts. Known as the Raging Horns Inn or Raging Horn Bull Inn.

chapter_mentions.context: Ainar mentions she stays there and offers help.
Bjorn finds Ainar at the Raging Horns Inn, a cheap inn housing many Barbarians.

information.label: Pricing and Conditions
information.notes: The inn is filled with Barbarians. The strong smell of sweat indicates crowded conditions.

name: Immortal Engraving
short_description: A high-rank Spirit Engraving on the Immortal Path. Costs 800,000 Stone but Bjorn received a 200,000 Stone gift from the Tribe Chief. Grants greatly increased natural regeneration and +20 to all physical stats. It is the key to Bjorn's Training Method. The procedure is performed by the Shaman with needles following tattoos. Stage 1 has been activated.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn underwent the Immortal Engraving Stage 1, greatly increasing his regeneration and physical stats.

information.label: Cost
information.notes: Bjorn plans to spend his 1.4 million Stone on this engraving, indicating it is very expensive.

information.label: Effects
information.notes: The engraving enhances the body with soul power. Bjorn experienced intense pain during the procedure, but felt a surge of energy afterward.

name: Spirit Engraving
short_description: A unique Barbarian growth method that infuses the body with Soul Power from magical ingredients via tattoos. All Barbarians receive it as newborns. It grants special powers and is performed by the Tribe Chief and Shaman. There are multiple paths; Bjorn chose the Immortal Path. The procedure is painful and involves needles following the existing tattoos.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn received a Spirit Engraving, learning it is an enhancement art using tattoos and soul power.

information.label: General Description
information.notes: Barbarians use Spirit Engraving, similar to Fairies handling spirits and Dwarves handling Artifacts. It is costly, but Bjorn plans to use his 1.4 million Stone for it.

information.label: Mechanics
information.notes: Infuses the body with Soul Power from magical ingredients. All Barbarians have tattoos from newborn age to keep soul circuits visible. The process is done by a Shaman using needles. pain is intense.

name: Tribe Chief
short_description: The leader of the Barbarian tribe at the Holy Land. He performs Spirit Engravings, gives discounts to promising warriors, and reacts positively to stories of tricking fairies. He is fiery and generous, giving Bjorn a 200,000 Stone discount. He is intimidating but can be pleased.

chapter_mentions.context: The Tribe Chief gave Bjorn a 200,000 Stone discount on the Immortal Engraving after hearing his tale.

information.label: Role
information.notes: Greets Bjorn and Ainar at the Holy Land. Performs Spirit Engravings. Bjorn thinks of him as 'this fucking bastard of a Tribe Chief', implying fear or dislike.

information.label: Personality
information.notes: Generous and fiery; laughed heartily at Bjorn's story about fleecing Erwen. Gave a 200,000 Stone discount as a gift. Bjorn found him intimidating at first but grew to like him.

name: Library
short_description: The massive library in Rafdonia where Bjorn studies daily from 8 AM to 4 PM. Librarian Parsitiev uses magic to locate books. It holds extensive knowledge on history, alchemy, monsters, and more.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn visits the Library to research; the librarian uses a spell to guide him to relevant books.
Bjorn established a daily routine of studying at the Library from 8 AM to 4 PM.

information.label: Magical Book Location

attributes.magic: spell that guides user to relevant books

information.notes: The librarian casts a spell that makes books relevant to the user's thoughts attract the user's attention.

information.label: Daily Use
information.notes: Bjorn studies daily from 8 AM to 4 PM. The librarian Parsitiev uses a locator spell to help find books. Bjorn has become accustomed to the magic.

name: Immortal Path
short_description: One of the paths for Spirit Engraving, chosen by Bjorn for its high-performance abilities and connection to his Training Method.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn chose the Immortal Path for his Spirit Engraving, noting its high performance and link to his Training Method.

information.label: Description
information.notes: Bjorn chose the Immortal Path for his Spirit Engraving, noting its high performance and link to his Training Method.

name: Commercial District
short_description: Shopping area in Rafdonia where Bjorn bought equipment including a mace, half armor, and a custom helmet.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn visited the Commercial District to invest his money in new equipment.

information.label: Shopping
information.notes: Bjorn visited the Commercial District to invest his money in new equipment. He bargained for a mace, bought a half armor that fit perfectly, and ordered a custom helmet.

name: Sacred Artifact War
short_description: Historical war mentioned in books, involving Spirit Engraving and the Fairy Tribe.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn mentions the Sacred Artifact War to the Tribe Chief, citing a book that describes Spirit Engraving's role in the war against the Fairy Tribe.

name: Shaman
short_description: Blindfolded Barbarian shaman who performs the Immortal Engraving ritual. He comments on Bjorn's clean soul circuits and uses needles to follow tattoos.

chapter_mentions.context: The blindfolded Shaman performed the Immortal Engraving on Bjorn, commenting on his clean soul circuits.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Blindfolded; performs the ritual with needles. Bjorn found the experience painful.

information.label: Role
information.notes: Performs the painful engraving procedure on warriors. He noted that Bjorn had clean circuits and that maintaining purity would allow spirits to grant power.

name: Dimensional Plaza
short_description: The plaza in Rafdonia where the Portal to the Labyrinth opens every month at midnight. Thousands of explorers gather to enter. The portal emits a light and shrinks over time.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Ainar waited in the Dimensional Plaza until the crowd thinned, then entered the portal as it was closing.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: A large plaza with a central point where the portal manifests as a shimmering orb of light. Explorers crowd in and enter when the official signals.

name: Land of the Dead
short_description: A region on the Second Floor of the Labyrinth, populated by undead monsters like Ghouls, Skeletons, Banshees, and Death Fiends.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Ainar hunt in the Land of the Dead on the Second Floor, encountering Banshees, Skeletons, and a Death Fiend.

information.label: Monsters and Environment
information.notes: Contains Ghouls, Elder Ghouls, Skeletons, Banshees, Dullahan, and Death Fiends. Higher-species (Eighth Rank) monsters appear deeper. Environment: black earth, ruins of stone buildings, faint mournful cries, eerie atmosphere.

name: Behel
short_description: The name of the ancestral spirit shouted by Barbarians during battle, symbolizing their battle cry.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Ainar shout 'Behel' as they charge into combat against the Death Fiend.

information.label: Usage
information.notes: Shouted as a war cry by Barbarians, e.g., 'Behel—laaaaaaa!!' when charging into battle.

name: Protein Blade and Shield
short_description: The names of the Death Fiend's arm-mounted sword and shield, which are part of its body.

chapter_mentions.context: The Death Fiend's Protein Blade and Shield are described during the battle.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: One arm is a long curved blade like a crescent moon, the other arm is shaped like a shield. Both are part of the Death Fiend's body.

name: Mark of Grudge
short_description: A curse inflicted by Banshees when attacked first, causing them to relentlessly target the cursed explorer within the Labyrinth.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn advises Ainar not to attack Banshees first to avoid the Mark of Grudge curse.

information.label: Effect
information.notes: If a Banshee is attacked first, it inflicts this curse, making the Banshee relentlessly target the victim until they leave the Labyrinth.

name: Call of the Dead
short_description: An active skill of the Death Fiend that summons Ghouls from the ground to aid it in battle.

chapter_mentions.context: The Death Fiend uses Call of the Dead during the fight, summoning Ghouls.

information.label: Effect
information.notes: The Death Fiend uses this skill to cause tremors and summon Ghouls (exactly ten) from beneath the ground to assist it.

name: Body Preservation
short_description: A passive skill of the Death Fiend that rapidly heals its wounds, making it difficult to kill without concentrated attacks.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn notes the Death Fiend's Body Preservation skill during the battle, explaining why its wounds heal quickly.

information.label: Effect
information.notes: Passive skill that stops bleeding within three seconds and rapidly regenerates flesh, rendering non-concentrated attacks ineffective.

name: Essence of the Death Fiend
short_description: A rare essence dropped by Death Fiends. Contains the Active Skill 'Call of the Dead' and the Passive Skill 'Body Preservation'. The passive is especially valuable for its synergy with the Immortal Engraving's regeneration boost.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn hoped to obtain this essence for its Body Preservation passive, which synergizes with his Immortal Engraving, but it did not drop after 70 kills.

name: Corpse Flowers
short_description: Flowers that bloom on vines covering the rubble of stone buildings in the Land of the Dead. When split open, they provide drinkable water.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn explained that Corpse Flowers are the only water source in the Land of the Dead, though Ainar was skeptical of their hygiene.

information.label: Usage
information.notes: Provides fresh water when split open; available at specific times (blooming cycle).

name: Blond Explorer
short_description: A human explorer with blond hair, approximately 160cm tall. Possesses a Gnome's Supernatural Ability that grants area stealth within a 3-meter radius on the ground. Captured by Bjorn and forced to use his ability to help them escape.

chapter_mentions.context: Captured by Bjorn and forced to activate his area stealth ability to evade the red-haired raider.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Small build.

name: Darvan
short_description: Bald human explorer who led a group of four explorers. Betrayed Bjorn and Ainar after offering help, using a Stone Golem's Suppression ability. Killed by Bjorn with a mace blow to the head.

chapter_mentions.context: Betrayed Bjorn and Ainar, subdued them with Suppression, then was killed by Bjorn in a desperate counterattack.

name: Ramod
short_description: Human explorer in Darvan's group. Urged to finish off Bjorn quickly; later killed by Ainar's greatsword.

chapter_mentions.context: One of the four explorers who betrayed Bjorn and Ainar; killed by Ainar after Bjorn freed her.

name: Red-haired Raider

aliases.name: Eighth Floor Raider
aliases.context: Revealed by Bjorn after she tells him she is from the Eighth Floor

short_description: A female raider from the Eighth Floor who specializes in killing other explorers in the Labyrinth. She has red hair, a tattoo under her eye, and the top half of her right ear missing. She uses a dagger, Basilisk Paralysis Poison, and carries a magical bag for storing loot. She owes a debt to Barbarians and forced Bjorn and Ainar to swear an Oath to keep silent, then healed them.

chapter_mentions.context: Encountered Bjorn and Ainar after killing four explorers, but did not attack them, choosing to ignore them after a brief exchange.
Continues to pursue Bjorn and Ainar after looting her victims, showing her persistence and confidence as a raider.
Forces Bjorn and Ainar to swear an Oath to keep silent, reveals she is from the Eighth Floor and owes a debt to Barbarians.

information.label: Appearance

attributes.distinguishing_marks: tattoo under eye, top half of right ear missing

information.notes: She wore no mask, indicating confidence in not being reported.

information.label: Profession
information.notes: Raider: hunts explorers for their gear rather than monsters. Her method likely involves poison, as three of the four corpses had no external injuries.

information.label: Equipment
information.notes: Carries a magical tool bag that can store bulky items; uses a bloodied dagger.

information.label: Combat Behavior
information.notes: Observed looting gear from corpses nonchalantly even with witnesses; gave chase but prioritized looting, indicating confidence and experience.

information.label: Basilisk Paralysis Poison
information.notes: Used to paralyze Bjorn and Ainar; described as causing the victim's muscles to stiffen and lose strength.

information.label: High-grade Potion
information.notes: Used a high-grade potion on Bjorn and Ainar to heal their wounds and lift the paralysis.

information.label: Debt to Barbarians
information.notes: Claims to owe a debt to Barbarians, which is why she offered an Oath instead of killing them.

name: Warrior's Oath
short_description: A sacred oath sworn by Barbarians on their Warrior's Honor. While it has no magical binding like a Fairy's promise, it carries significant cultural weight and is generally trusted by others.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Ainar swear the Warrior's Oath to the Eighth Floor Raider to avoid death.

name: Basilisk Paralysis Poison
short_description: A poison that causes paralysis in its victim. Used by the Eighth Floor Raider. The poison is delivered via a small needle or dagger, and a high-grade potion is required to counteract it.

chapter_mentions.context: The Eighth Floor Raider uses it to paralyze Bjorn and Ainar; Ainar struck in the wrist, Bjorn in the spine.

name: Monument to the Great Sage
short_description: A stone tablet in the center of the First Floor, used to open Rifts by offering Magic Stones of four monster types and an Eighth-Rank stone. Inscription honors the Last Great Sage, Difflun Groundel Gabrielius.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Bjorn to forcibly open a Rift.

information.label: Function
information.notes: To open a Rift, offer a Magic Stone from each of Goblin, Gnome, Ghoul, and Blade Wolf, plus an Eighth-Rank Magic Stone. The stone disappears and a portal opens.

name: Frost Lord's Palace
short_description: A Rift on the Eighth Floor, known to be extremely challenging. Referenced by Ainar as a place she has heard of.

chapter_mentions.context: Mentioned by Ainar as an example of a Rift she knows about from stories.

name: Death Fiend
short_description: An eighth-rank undead monster found in the Land of the Dead. It stands over three meters tall, with one arm shaped like a blade and the other like a shield. It possesses high defense, rapid regeneration (Body Preservation), and can summon Ghouls (Call of the Dead). Its Magic Stone can be used as a sacrifice to open the Bloodstained Fortress Rift on the First Floor.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Ainar hunted over 70 Death Fiends over two days on the second floor, using a coordinated takedown strategy, but did not obtain its rare Essence.
Its Magic Stone is used as a sacrifice to open the Bloodstained Fortress Rift.

information.label: Appearance

attributes.arms: Protein Blade and Shield (part of body)

information.notes: A humanoid undead with a distorted form; its arms are natural weapons.

information.label: Abilities
information.notes: The regeneration makes it difficult to kill without focused attacks to the head.

information.label: Combat Strategy
information.notes: Vulnerable to takedowns due to poor balance; can be overcome by two Barbarians working together to slam it to the ground and continuously attack the head.

information.label: Drop
information.notes: May rarely drop an Essence containing the Call of the Dead and Body Preservation skills.

name: Blackrock Mountain
short_description: A hunting area on the Second Floor, originally planned destination for hunting Gnomes, Kobolds, and Stone Golems before Bjorn decided to enter a Rift instead.

chapter_mentions.context: Mentioned as the original planned hunting ground that Bjorn decides to forgo.

name: Last Great Sage Difflun Groundel Gabrielius
short_description: The historical figure honored by the Monument to the Great Sage, mentioned in the inscription on the monument.

chapter_mentions.context: Inscription on the monument states 'In honor of the great first step of the Last Great Sage, Difflun Groundel Gabrielius'.

name: Rift
short_description: A portal that opens randomly on each floor of the Labyrinth, leading to a separate dungeon space (instance dungeon). Rifts have a cooldown of at least three cycles (months). They can be forcibly opened by offering specific magic stones at the Monument to the Great Sage. Rifts contain a Guardian that drops special items such as Numbers Items, Guardian's Essence, and Rift Stones.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Ainar enter a Rift on the First Floor by using the Monument to the Great Sage, aiming to gain strength.
The Rift expedition into the Bloodstained Fortress involved negotiation over the distribution of these spoils.

information.label: Cooldown
information.notes: Rifts open randomly between 3 and 8 cycles after the last one.

information.label: Loot
information.notes: These items are highly sought after by explorers.

name: Numbers Item
short_description: A valuable item from the Guardian's spoils in a Rift, specifically desired by the dwarf Murad.

chapter_mentions.context: Murad insisted on taking the Numbers Item from the Guardian's spoils.

name: Rift Stone
short_description: An item from a Rift that can be used for loot allocation; Bjorn gave it up in exchange for priority on other essences.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn initially demanded the Rift Stone but later exchanged it for priority on essences.

name: Underground Prison
short_description: The third chapter of the Bloodstained Fortress. A maze-like area consisting of three floors, reminiscent of a mad scientist's laboratory. Contains monsters such as Skull Rats, Banshees, Death Fiends, Chimera Wolves, Ghoul Lords, and Corpse Golems.

chapter_mentions.context: The party descends into the Underground Prison, the third chapter of the Bloodstained Fortress, encountering the Corpse Golem.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: The first floor has iron bars with chained dead men and skeletal rats. The second floor resembles a laboratory with chimera wolves and ghoul lords. The final floor is a circular, sewage-like space with large pipes dripping blackish filth and a floor piled with bent limbs and heads.

name: Flame Baptism
short_description: A fire spell used by Raven that fills a straight corridor with flames, destroying a group of Banshees.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Raven to clear the corridor in the Underground Prison, instantly killing all Banshees.

name: Blade Storm
short_description: A wind spell used by Raven that creates a fierce gale, shredding multiple enemies over a wide area.

chapter_mentions.context: Though not used in this chapter, it was previously employed to clear the bridge and outer walls in the Bloodstained Fortress.

name: Curse Dispelling
short_description: A spell used by Raven to remove curses, such as petrification from Gargoyle Statues.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Raven to lift the petrification curse during the Gargoyle encounter.

name: Wound aggravation curse
short_description: A curse spell used by Raven to weaken enemies by aggravating their wounds, sealing passive abilities like Body Preservation.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Raven against Death Fiends to remove their regeneration ability, allowing easier kills.

name: Mana Arrow
short_description: A basic attack spell used by Raven, firing an arrow of light with no casting time.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Raven to mark a Banshee, triggering the Mark of Grudge.

name: Corpse Golem
short_description: The mid-boss of the Bloodstained Fortress Underground Prison. A Rank 7 elite monster made of corpses, it speaks in a female voice expressing pain and fragmented emotions. It attacks by throwing exploding corpses and pulling enemies in with hundreds of arms. It has two phases: an outer shell that can be burned away, and a Bone Golem mode with faster movements. Its weak point is a magic core inside its ribs.

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as the mid-boss of the Bloodstained Fortress, it attacks the party in its lair after they enter the third underground floor.
Defeated by Bjorn and Ainar after Raven's Flame Baptism burned away its outer shell; dropped a Corpse Golem Essence.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: A massive pile of human arms, legs, and heads packed together; each part moves individually. Hundreds of mouths spew uncontrolled, fragmented emotions such as 'it hurts', 'kill me', 'save me'.

information.label: Attack
information.notes: Throws piles of corpses like a shot put, which explode on impact, scattering flesh and blood. The fluid is acidic and can cause injuries like eye damage.

information.label: Combat Abilities
information.notes: Fought in the Bloodstained Fortress Underground Prison. After outer shell is burned away, it enters Bone Golem mode with faster movements. Defeated by breaking ribs to expose core.

name: Tarjin
short_description: A professional Porter hired by Arua Raven to assist in the Rift. He is a human man. After the Corpse Golem battle, he blocked Bjorn and Ainar from approaching Raven, showing distrust.

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as the porter accompanying Mage Raven into the Rift.
Blocked the party after the Corpse Golem fight, warning them not to come closer.

information.label: Loyalty
information.notes: Remained by Raven's side and blocked Bjorn and Ainar from approaching after the battle, showing distrust of outsiders.

name: Guardian's Essence
short_description: A special essence dropped by the Guardian of a Rift, claimed by Raven in the loot negotiation. In the Bloodstained Fortress, Raven agreed to trade this essence for the Corpse Golem Essence with Bjorn.

chapter_mentions.context: Raven claimed the Guardian's Essence as part of the loot agreement.
Raven agreed to trade this essence for the Corpse Golem essence.

name: Demon Worship Hall
short_description: The final chapter of the Bloodstained Fortress Rift, a spacious stone chamber with intimidating demon sculptures and a floor covered in human skulls. Located beneath the lord's castle.

chapter_mentions.context: The party enters the Demon Worship Hall as the final area of the Rift.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: A spacious stone chamber with demon sculptures and a floor covered in human skulls.

name: Corpse Golem Essence
short_description: The essence dropped by the Corpse Golem in the Bloodstained Fortress. Contains stats: Pain Resistance+70, Bone Density+55, Poison Resistance+12, Strength+15, Perception-5, Appetite+9, Weight+21. Special skills: (P) Acidic Fluid - blood becomes acidic, damaging equipment when wounded; (A) Flesh Explosion - detonate body part at HP cost.

chapter_mentions.context: Dropped by the Corpse Golem; Bjorn traded it to Raven in exchange for the Guardian's Essence.

information.label: Stats and Skills
information.notes: Passive: Acidic Fluid makes blood acidic, damaging equipment when wounded. Active: Flesh Explosion deals damage but costs HP.

name: Bloodstained Fortress
short_description: A Rift (instance dungeon) on the First Floor, entered by offering magic stones at the Monument to the Great Sage. It has a red sky and a dark fortress in the distance. The fortress consists of an outer checkpoint with a drawbridge and moat, followed by three chapters: city streets of the outer wall, underground prison of the inner wall, and the Demon Worship Hall in the lord's castle. Monsters include Gargoyle Statues, Dead men, Skeleton Archers, Ghouls, and Death Fiends.

chapter_mentions.context: Opened by Bjorn and Ainar as the first Rift they enter.
The party entered and cleared the outer checkpoint and city streets, encountering various undead and Gargoyle Statues.
Confirmed that the Guardian of the Bloodstained Fortress can vary; encountered Vampire Duke Cambormir instead of the expected Death Knight.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Red sky, flock of crows, dark fortress visible in the distance.

information.label: Dungeon Layout
information.notes: Described in detail as the Bloodstained Fortress.

information.label: Monster Types
information.notes: Includes Gargoyle Statues, Dead men, Skeleton Archers, Ghouls, and Death Fiends.

information.label: Guardian
information.notes: The Guardian changed unexpectedly; Vampire Duke Cambormir is a Rank 5 Advanced Mutant boss.

name: Guardian
short_description: The boss monster of the Bloodstained Fortress Rift, which can vary. In one instance, it was expected to be a Death Knight but actually appeared as the vampire Duke Cambormir, a Rank 5 Advanced Mutant.

chapter_mentions.context: Raven demands the spoils from the Guardian for her own research.
The Guardian of the Bloodstained Fortress was revealed to be Vampire Duke Cambormir instead of the anticipated Death Knight.

information.label: Encountered Form
information.notes: The Guardian changed unexpectedly in the current expedition.

name: Necronomicon
short_description: Dark Magic book found by Raven in the stone chamber of the Bloodstained Fortress Demon Worship Hall. Can be taken outside without Distortion Magic.

chapter_mentions.context: Discovered by Raven using a hidden mechanism, claimed by her as spoils.

information.label: Properties
information.notes: Contains Dark Magic; treated as an item for inventory purposes.

name: Bone Knight Essence
short_description: Rank 7 monster essence with Yearning attribute, grants Life Drain ability and reduces height. Used by Ainar in Chapter 32.

chapter_mentions.context: Dropped by a Bone Knight in the Demon Worship Hall, claimed by Bjorn and given to Ainar.

information.label: Stats
information.notes: Yearning type: only has Life Drain ability; Preservative passive reduces wound-worsening effects by half.

name: Vampire Duke Cambormir
short_description: An Advanced Mutant vampire and the Guardian of the Bloodstained Fortress. Possesses Dark Magic including Wraith Form, Materialization, Sacrificial Mark, and Pain Sharing. Uses claws, turns into crows, and can regenerate health via Immortal One. A Fifth Rank monster with high combat power. Defeated by Bjorn and his party in a hard-fought battle; his essence was absorbed by Bjorn.

chapter_mentions.context: Replaces the Death Knight as the Guardian of the Bloodstained Fortress, surprising the party with his intelligence and power.
Pursues the party after they open the wrong door, using Dark Magic and the Pain Sharing ability gained from an Essence.
Serves as the Guardian of the Bloodstained Fortress, engaging the party in a fierce battle while using Sacrificial Mark and Pain Sharing.
Engages Bjorn's party in a prolonged battle, using Pain Sharing and Immortal One to survive, but ultimately is killed by Bjorn's relentless use of Flesh Explosion.

information.label: Combat Abilities
information.notes: Proficient in close combat and Dark Magic; possesses intelligence and a history.

information.label: Intelligent Named Monster
information.notes: Has a name and history; speaks to the party upon awakening.

information.label: Abilities
information.notes: Also possesses Pain Sharing ability from an Essence.

information.label: Skills

attributes.Sacrificial Mark: Buff skill that designates sacrifices and increases physical abilities based on number of sacrifices within 100m
attributes.Pain Sharing: Active skill that shares damage received with the attacker

information.notes: Uses Wraith Form to pursue, transforms into crows that explode, and combines Dark Magic with close combat.

information.label: Abilities Used

attributes.Immortal_One: Greatly increases regeneration proportional to lost health
attributes.Sacrificial_Mark: Designates sacrifices within 100m to increase physical abilities

information.notes: The Vampire Duke's Immortal One made him extremely difficult to kill, as his wounds healed rapidly. His Pain Sharing ability incapacitated the entire party after Raven's Black Sun Orb exploded. He was finally defeated when Bjorn destroyed his heart with repeated Flesh Explosions.

name: Black Sun Orb
short_description: A sixth-rank attack spell with the sun attribute, combining fire and holy attributes. Less powerful than a fireball of same rank. Cast by Arua Raven.

chapter_mentions.context: Cast by Raven to damage the Vampire Duke, but the explosion also caught the party, stunning them.

name: Pain Sharing
short_description: A spell cast by the Vampire Duke Cambormir that shares pain with the target, causing them to feel the vampire's injuries. Used to incapacitate the party.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by the vampire after being hit by Black Sun Orb, it stunned Murad, Ainar, and the Porter.

name: Immortal One
short_description: A passive effect that greatly increases the vampire's regeneration in proportion to lost health. Possessed by the Vampire Duke Cambormir and later granted to Bjorn via his essence.

chapter_mentions.context: Demonstrated by the vampire as it regenerated from critical wounds; later acquired by Bjorn.

name: Source of Darkness
short_description: A passive skill obtained from the Vampire Guardian Essence that prevents the character from dying until their heart is destroyed.

chapter_mentions.context: Acquired by Bjorn after absorbing the Vampire Guardian Essence, ensuring his survival even after reaching 0% HP.

name: Lightning Discharge
short_description: A rare transformation-type Supernatural Ability used by Hikurod Murad. Allows the user to release energy gained through other abilities to the outside. Used in combination with 'Lightning' to stun enemies.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Murad to stun the Vampire Duke, creating an opening for Bjorn.

name: Attribute Enhancement
short_description: A spell cast by Arua Raven to enhance the power of Black Sun Orb, increasing its effectiveness.

chapter_mentions.context: Cast by Raven to boost the power of Black Sun Orb before firing.

name: Rafdonia
short_description: The fortress city where explorers are transported after the Labyrinth closes. It is a bustling, modern-style city with tall stone buildings, plumbing, and a structured society with law and order. The city is vast (crossing takes over an hour on foot). It has a Central Plaza where tax evaders are publicly executed by guillotine. There is a superstition that drinking the blood of executed tax evaders brings good fortune with money. The first-year tax for non-humans is 700,000 Stone, increasing thereafter. The city also houses an Exchange Office for Ninth-Rank Explorers, where magic stones can be exchanged for currency; the office is equipped with an emergency button to summon guards, as demonstrated when Bjorn was arrested upon suspicion.

chapter_mentions.context: Revealed as the city where explorers are sent when the labyrinth closes; Bjorn and Erwen plan to meet at the Black Whale Tavern there.
Described in the daytime as a peaceful, modern-feeling city with stone buildings, running water, and a strong sense of law and order, contrasting with the danger of the Labyrinth.
Bjorn and Erwen explore the city, sell loot, and witness executions of tax evaders at the Central Plaza.
Bjorn visits the Exchange Office for Ninth-Rank Explorers to exchange Magic Stones, but is unexpectedly arrested by guards after the official becomes suspicious.

information.label: Transportation City
information.notes: Explorers are moved here when the labyrinth closes each cycle.

information.label: Appearance

attributes.architecture: tall stone buildings, mostly over three stories
attributes.amenities: running water from faucets, modern atmosphere

information.notes: The main road is bustling with people; buildings are made of stone and often three or more stories high. Water comes from faucets, reminiscent of modern cities. The city appears orderly and safe.

name: Exchange Office for Ninth-Rank Explorers
short_description: A government office in Rafdonia where Ninth-Rank Explorers exchange Magic Stones for currency. Staffed by a public official who can summon guards via a hidden emergency button under the desk.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn goes to this office to exchange his Magic Stones, but the official becomes suspicious and calls guards, leading to Bjorn's arrest.

name: Flesh Explosion
short_description: A Supernatural Ability (or Skill) that allows the user to detonate their own flesh. Bjorn used it during the fight with the Vampire Duke, repeatedly to break his cell bars, melt the interrogation room door, and blast the vampire's chest until its heart was destroyed.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn casts Flesh Explosion to begin his escape from the Guild's prison and exact revenge.
Used repeatedly to break iron bars, melt a door, and damage the vampire's chest during the prison break.

information.label: Usage
information.notes: Bjorn casts Flesh Explosion at the end of the chapter to escape imprisonment, planning to make the Guild pay.

name: Sacrificial Mark
short_description: A buff skill originally used by Vampire Duke Cambormir, now acquired by Bjorn via the Vampire essence. It marks targets within a 100m radius with a glowing red tattoo on the forehead and temporarily increases the user's physical abilities based on the number of sacrifices. The mark is applied through contact with the user's acidic blood.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn uses the skill during the prison riot, marking dozens of people with his acidic blood to boost his physical abilities.

name: Julian Urbans
short_description: Daughter of the Regional Chief; taken hostage by Bjorn during his escape from the Explorer's Guild prison. Remains calm and tries to negotiate with Bjorn, showing a strong will.

chapter_mentions.context: Taken hostage by Bjorn to improve his bargaining position and force the Guild's cooperation.

information.label: Composure Under Pressure
information.notes: Despite being taken hostage, Julian Urbans remained calm, assessed Bjorn's behavior, and attempted to negotiate, showing a strong will and quick thinking.

name: Temple
short_description: A religious institution in Rafdonia. Bjorn demands an official Priest from the Temple to be brought to verify his innocence.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn demands an official Priest from the Temple to be brought for verification.

name: Explorer's Guild Branch Chief
short_description: The branch chief of the Rafdonia Explorer's Guild. A former explorer. He is subdued by Bjorn during his escape and forced to comply with demands at knifepoint, with the Regional Chief's daughter as hostage. In the current chapter, he is forced to summon the Investigator, Hartz Young, and a Mage, and to cooperate with Bjorn's plan for a public trial.

chapter_mentions.context: Subdued by Bjorn and forced to cooperate by calling off guards and summoning specific people.
Forced to act as Bjorn's puppet, he summons key individuals and cooperates under duress.

information.label: Strategic Compliance
information.notes: After being subdued and with the Regional Chief's daughter as hostage, the Branch Chief complies with Bjorn's demands to minimize further harm, showing pragmatism.

name: Rafdonia Royal Family
short_description: The royal family of Rafdonia. They have delegated significant authority related to the Labyrinth to the Explorer's Guild, but strictly adhere to the principle of executing Raiders. Mentioned in the context of hostage negotiation limitations.

chapter_mentions.context: Referenced as the authority that limits the Guild's ability to negotiate with hostage-takers.

name: Recording Magic
short_description: A type of magic that records events for evidence. Used by Arua Raven to document the interrogation and events in the Branch Chief's office.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Raven to record the proceedings, ensuring the truth is captured for later verification.

name: Niall Urbans
short_description: Regional Chief of the Explorer's Guild, second-in-command below the Guild Master. He is forced to negotiate with Bjorn Yandel after the prison break and hostage crisis, agreeing to dismiss the investigator, sentence Hartz Young to death, promote Bjorn to Rank 7, and pay 5 million Stones in compensation.

chapter_mentions.context: Forced to negotiate with Bjorn Yandel after the prison break and hostage crisis, ultimately agreeing to all demands.

information.label: Political Pragmatism
information.notes: Attempts to bribe Arua Raven to sell the Crystal Orb, then concedes to all demands when the recording is used against him.

name: Execution Ground
short_description: The location where criminals are executed, mentioned as Hartz Young's likely fate.

chapter_mentions.context: Referenced as the destination for Hartz Young after his sentencing.

name: Explorer's Guild
short_description: An organization in Rafdonia that provides services to Explorers. Recent internal regulations target low-rank explorers; Investigator Coldo Bierman unjustly sentenced Bjorn Yandel to death. After Bjorn Yandel's prison break and hostage crisis, the Guild was forced to dismiss the corrupt investigator, sentence Hartz Young to death, compensate Bjorn with 5 million Stones and a rank promotion, and investigate the Crystal Alliance. The incident tarnished its reputation.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn and Ainar visited the Explorer's Guild to receive a Binding Spell before entering the Labyrinth.
The Guild, through corrupt Investigator Coldo Bierman, unjustly sentenced Bjorn to death, revealing internal corruption and recent regulatory changes.
The branch is in chaos as Bjorn holds the Branch Chief hostage and demands a public trial, drawing a crowd of onlookers.
Forced to publicly admit its mistakes and compensate Bjorn Yandel after the hostage crisis.

information.label: Services
information.notes: Offers Binding Spell to allow party members to start in the same location upon entering the Labyrinth. The fee increases with higher ranks.

information.label: Notable Incident
information.notes: Investigator Coldo Bierman railroaded Bjorn Yandel on false raiding charges, denied verification, and sentenced him to death, revealing internal corruption and recent regulatory changes.

information.label: Organizational Response
information.notes: Dismissed investigator Coldo Bierman, sentenced Hartz Young to Execution Ground, and paid 5 million Stones compensation to Bjorn Yandel.

name: Coldo Bierman
short_description: An Investigator for the Explorer's Guild in Rafdonia. Corrupt and overbearing, he handled Bjorn Yandel's case, ignoring evidence, denying verification, and sentencing him to death. He is dismissed from his position and faces a thorough investigation into his corruption, orchestrated by the Regional Chief.

chapter_mentions.context: Corrupt Investigator who railroaded Bjorn Yandel, sentencing him to death based on flimsy evidence.
Beaten and knocked out by Bjorn during the prison break.
Brought to the Branch Chief's office, he defends his investigation but is confronted about the lack of magical verification for the testimony.
Dismissed and under investigation for corruption after his framing of Bjorn Yandel is exposed.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Overbearing way of speaking, grins slyly after kicking Bjorn.

information.label: Behavior
information.notes: Abuses his authority, ignores due process, uses physical intimidation (kicked Bjorn's shin), determined to get a confession by any means.

information.label: Dismissal
information.notes: Regional Chief Niall Urbans orders his dismissal and a thorough investigation into his corruption.

name: Hartz Young
short_description: A member of the Crystal Alliance clan who gave false testimony against Bjorn Yandel, leading to Bjorn's death sentence. He is sentenced to death at the Execution Ground after being exposed for false testimony.

chapter_mentions.context: Raises the bounty on Erwen to 20,000 Stone after being injured.
Beaten and imprisoned in a wardrobe by Bjorn for giving false testimony that resulted in Bjorn's death sentence.
Sentenced to Execution Ground after his false testimony was exposed during the trial.

information.label: Bounty
information.notes: Offers a reward and the chance to have Erwen first to the one who captures her.

information.label: Sentencing
information.notes: Regional Chief orders his execution for false testimony.

name: Vampire Guardian Essence
short_description: The Essence dropped by the Vampire Duke Cambormir after his defeat. Grants stat increases (Strength +15, Agility +15, Magic Resistance +30, Natural Regeneration +40) and the passive skills Source of Darkness and Immortal One. Absorbed by Bjorn.

chapter_mentions.context: Obtained by Bjorn after the Vampire Duke's death, it saves his life by restoring HP and granting new abilities.
Referenced as a reason for Bjorn's quick healing during the prison break.

name: Altemion School
short_description: The mage school to which Arua Raven belongs.

chapter_mentions.context: Revealed as the school of Arua Raven.

name: Crystal Orb
short_description: A magical communication device that allows calling and talking to an explorer, used by Arua Raven.

chapter_mentions.context: Used by Raven to contact Mr. Patran to verify the consequences of forcing an Essence.

name: Mr. Patran
short_description: An explorer contacted via Crystal Orb by Arua Raven. He confirms that forcing an unplanned Essence onto someone requires compensation of 15 million Stone or death.

chapter_mentions.context: Queried by Raven about the consequences of forcing an Essence, providing a clear answer that supports Bjorn's argument.

name: Mage Tower
short_description: An organization of mages in Rafdonia. Bjorn demands a mage from the tower to be brought to verify his innocence. In this chapter, the Mage Tower dispatches Sixth-Rank Mage Arua Raven to support the branch in response to an official request. It is a tall black spire with 31 floors, windowless, located in the city. Arua Raven's personal lab is on the 31st floor.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn demands a mage from the Mage Tower to be brought for verification purposes.
Responds to the branch's request for support by sending a Sixth-Rank Mage.
Mentioned as the tall black spire where Arua Raven's personal lab is located on the 31st floor.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: A black spire soaring high into the sky, built high due to regulations prohibiting windows in tall buildings in the city.

name: Deian Tveherion
short_description: Master of the Altemion School, a senior mage with white hair and wrinkled skin but spry. He enters Raven's lab and offers the Frost Spirit Ring as compensation to Bjorn. He has a jovial personality but can be calculating.

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as Raven's master, who offers the Frost Spirit Ring and later reflects on Bjorn's peculiarity.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: White-haired old man with wrinkled skin, back not bent, looks remarkably spry for his age.

information.label: Jovial Personality
information.notes: Laughs heartily, makes jokes about dead barbarians, and engages in banter with Bjorn.

information.label: Calculating Nature
information.notes: Had a purpose for matching Bjorn's high tension, later reveals he wanted to confirm something about Bjorn.

name: Frost Spirit Ring
short_description: A Numbers Item (no. 9425) that can seal the skills of one absorbed Essence while retaining stats. Rare, worth about 1.5 million Stones. Given by Deian to Bjorn as compensation.

chapter_mentions.context: Offered by Deian as a substitute for 5 million Stones compensation; Bjorn accepts it.

information.label: Effect

attributes.effect: Seals skills of one absorbed Essence, stats still apply

information.notes: Used by early-stage explorers who value stats over skill combinations; can be removed to reactivate skills.

name: Holy Land
short_description: A communal child-rearing facility for Barbarians, where children are sent by parents who are warriors. Also a place where Barbarians go to learn from elders (e.g., learning to write) and to receive Spirit Engravings from the Tribe Chief. This chapter reveals its role in performing Spirit Engravings. Additionally, it is a sacred training ground where Ainar will train under an elder for six months.

chapter_mentions.context: Referred to as the place where non-humans grow up until their coming of age, explaining the significance of the first return ale.
Ainar plans to visit the Holy Land to learn to read from the elders.
Bjorn and Ainar visit the Holy Land to meet the Tribe Chief for the Spirit Engraving.
Ainar decides to train here for six months under an elder, learning a special swordsmanship technique.

information.label: Function for Barbarians
information.notes: Serves as a place for Barbarians to learn to read and to receive Spirit Engravings. The Tribe Chief resides there and performs the engravings. Also functions as a child-rearing facility.

information.label: Training Ground
information.notes: Ainar decides to train here for six months under an elder, learning a special swordsmanship technique, making her unavailable for Labyrinth expeditions.

name: Ainar
short_description: A female Barbarian explorer, second daughter of Fenelin, who partners with Bjorn. She absorbed the Bone Knight Essence, gaining increased cutting power and Life Drain. In the Vampire Duke battle, she assists by drawing aggro and supporting Murad. After the battle, she stayed with Bjorn for three days while he was unconscious, giving him a lap pillow, clothing him with a makeshift skirt, and insisting on waiting for him to wake up while the others left. In Chapter 43, she decides to formally learn swordsmanship under an elder at the Holy Land for six months, leaving Bjorn alone.

chapter_mentions.context: Encountered Bjorn in the Library; she plans to learn to read at the Holy Land and offered help at the Raging Horn Bull Inn.
After sparring, Ainar agrees to become Bjorn's companion with an 8:2 split in his favor.
Began daily sparring sessions with Ainar, observing her predictable fighting patterns.
Became Bjorn's loyal companion, pledged loyalty by Warrior's Honor, and followed him into the Labyrinth using the Binding Spell.
Ainar fights alongside Bjorn in the Land of the Dead, showing fear of Banshees but bravery against the Death Fiend, executing a takedown and dealing damage.
Reluctantly swears the Oath to save her life, struggles with the humiliation of being spared.
Insists that Bjorn should lead the party instead of the Mage, praising his wisdom and loyalty.
Absorbs the Bone Knight Essence, becoming significantly stronger but shorter, and worrying about her warrior image.
Assists Bjorn in the final battle against the Vampire Duke, drawing aggro and supporting the wounded Murad.
Cared for the unconscious Bjorn for three days, providing comfort and clothing, and remained with him until he woke up.
Decides to formally learn swordsmanship under an elder at the Holy Land for six months, leaving Bjorn alone.

information.label: Determination to Learn
information.notes: Realized she needed knowledge to think; found reading difficult but decided to go to the Holy Land to learn from elders.

information.label: Combat Skills
information.notes: Sparred with Bjorn multiple times; beat him within 3 minutes each time. Considered among the top ranks of Barbarians in the group.

information.label: Companion Agreement
information.notes: Accepted Bjorn's offer to be his companion with an 8:2 split (Bjorn 80%, Ainar 20%), trusting Bjorn with the distribution.

information.label: Combat Style
information.notes: Skilled swordsman with a predictable pattern that Bjorn exploited. She is open to advice but struggles to change ingrained habits. Her sparring is intense and sweaty.

information.label: Character Traits
information.notes: Pledged loyalty to Bjorn by Warrior's Honor; previously ate only Stone Bread; now enjoys normal food; follows Bjorn without question; altruistic - helped lost barbarians navigate to the Labyrinth.

information.label: Fear Response
information.notes: Shows fear of supernatural undead like Banshees, clenching her jaw and clinging to Bjorn when frightened, but manages to fight bravely.

information.label: Combat Prowess
information.notes: Demonstrated ability to perform a single-leg takedown on a 3-meter tall Death Fiend, enabling Bjorn to strike its head. Her Greatsword can cleave through skeletons and inflict significant damage on eighth-rank monsters.

information.label: Pride and Humiliation
information.notes: Ainar is deeply humiliated by having to swear the Oath, stating that 'for someone who is no longer a warrior, there is no right to feel pain.' She struggles to overcome her pride.

information.label: Composure in Crisis
information.notes: Stayed calm when the party was stunned by the Corpse Golem's appearance, grabbing the Dwarf by the scruff of his neck and lifting him out of harm's way, saving him from an explosive corpse attack.

information.label: Post-Essence Stats
information.notes: Absorbed Bone Knight Essence (Yearning type); gained Life Drain active ability and Preservative passive.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Height decreased; facial bones became smaller; now could pass for a tall human woman without tattoos.

information.label: Training Decision
information.notes: Decides to train under an elder at the Holy Land for six months, mastering a special swordsmanship technique, and cannot enter the Labyrinth during that time.

name: Primordial Relic
short_description: One of six key items needed to open the Gate of the Abyss; recently stolen on the day of Bjorn and Ainar's Coming of Age Ceremony, blocking Bjorn's potential escape from the world.

chapter_mentions.context: Revealed to have been stolen on the day of Bjorn and Ainar's Coming of Age Ceremony, blocking potential escape from the world.

information.label: Key Item
information.notes: The Primordial Relics are kept by the six tribes (Dwarf, Barbarian, Fairy, Beastkin, Dragonkin, Human). The theft occurred on the day of the Coming of Age Ceremony, and it is unknown which other tribes may have been affected.

name: Barbarian Inn
short_description: Inn exclusive to Barbarians in Rafdonia where Bjorn sought Ainar and heard rumors of the Barbarian of Freedom.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn visited to find Ainar but learned she had left for the Holy Land.

name: Commelby
short_description: Free market area surrounding the Imperial Capital Karnon, also known as the Commercial District covering Zones 2 to 5. It is the most commercially active place in the city, home to the middle class, artisans, and shops.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn visited with Ainar to sell equipment and buy items.

information.label: Commercial Hub

attributes.location: Zones 2 to 5 around Imperial Capital Karnon

information.notes: Bjorn visited with Ainar to sell equipment and buy items. Crowded and bustling.

name: Arua Raven

aliases.name: Raven
aliases.context: Common form of her name used by her party.

short_description: A Sixth-Rank Mage of the Mage Tower who joins Bjorn's party for the Rift expedition. She is proud, loves to show off, and is passionate about magic and research. She uses spells such as Flash Explosion, Black Sun Orb, etc. After the Vampire Duke fight, she took the Rift Stone and Necronomicon. She is dispatched by the Mage Tower to support the Guild branch, uses recording magic to document the interrogation, and secures a 1 million Stones service fee by blackmailing the Regional Chief. She is a member of the Altemion School. After the Guild incident, she proposes exclusive research on Bjorn's Vampire Essence and bargains for a share of the compensation. Later, she brings Bjorn to her lab and becomes uneasy when her master Deian shows unusual interest in Bjorn. In this chapter, she examines Bjorn's Essence, reveals its four skills, and begins to suspect Bjorn used the Tear of the Goddess, researching it in her book and becoming determined to uncover his secret.

chapter_mentions.context: Joins the Rift party and immediately asserts leadership, claiming the Guardian's spoils for herself.
Negotiated loot distribution and provided crucial magical support during the battles in the Bloodstained Fortress.
Though she led the party effectively, Raven showed vulnerability to the putrid smell of the Corpse Golem's lair, vomiting alongside the porter.
Suffered a backlash after forcing herself to cast a spell while nauseated, but later agreed to trade the Corpse Golem Essence for the Guardian's Essence.
Demonstrates her knowledge by finding a hidden compartment with a Necronomicon and explaining the Bone Knight Essence's effects.
Uses Flash Explosion to blind the vampire, then later faints from Pain Sharing backlash; reveals she knows Black Sun Orb and plans to enhance it.
Casts Black Sun Orb to damage the Vampire Duke, but the explosion also catches the party, causing them to be stunned.
After the battle, she took the Necronomicon and Rift Stone, used potions on Bjorn, and waited three days before leaving through the portal.

Sent to the branch as support, she uses recording magic to document the interrogation and reveals the Investigator's statements are truthful, but challenges the lack of magical confirmation for the testimony.
Uses recording magic to document the interrogation and negotiates a 1 million Stones service fee for herself.
Proposes exclusive research on Bjorn's Vampire Essence and negotiates for a share of compensation.
Brings Bjorn to her lab and becomes worried after her master Deian shows unusual interest in him.
Examines Bjorn's Essence, reveals its four skills, then researches the Tear of the Goddess, becoming suspicious of Bjorn's secrets.

information.label: Rank
information.notes: Class: Mage

information.label: Personality
information.notes: Pretends to be humble but loves to show off; passionate about magic and research; responds positively to admiration.

information.label: Magic Skills
information.notes: Demonstrated during the Bloodstained Fortress expedition, including area-of-effect and curse spells.

information.label: Weakness: Strong Odors
information.notes: Vomited from the stench in the Corpse Golem's lair, despite being resistant to gory sights from her anatomical studies.

information.label: Vulnerability to Strong Odors
information.notes: Vomited from the stench of the Corpse Golem's lair, leading to a spellcasting backlash (circuit severance) that nearly incapacitated her.

information.label: Arcane Knowledge
information.notes: Knows about hidden compartments in the Demon Worship Hall and the properties of the Bone Knight Essence, including its Life Drain and Height reduction effects.

information.label: Spells
information.notes: Uses Flash Explosion to blind enemies. Plans to cast Attribute Enhancement on Black Sun Orb.

information.label: Spells Used

attributes.Black_Sun_Orb: Sixth-rank attack magic with sun attribute
attributes.Attribute_Enhancement: Enhances the power of Black Sun Orb

information.notes: Raven completed the casting of Black Sun Orb after preparing for several minutes. She also used Attribute Enhancement to boost its power. The spell successfully hit the vampire but also stunned the party due to the explosion.

information.label: Negotiation Skill
information.notes: Blackmailed the Regional Chief with the recorded conversation to secure a 1 million Stones service fee.

name: Tear of the Goddess
short_description: A one-time use Sacred Artifact of unknown origin. When destroyed, it spreads a powerful 'Grace' at that location. Used 150 years ago by Cardinal Androne to save the Pope, after which it vanished from the world. Bjorn retrieved one from a broken fountain statue in the Bloodstained Fortress courtyard and used it against the Vampire Duke. Raven suspects Bjorn possesses it and is investigating.

chapter_mentions.context: Retrieved earlier and kept hidden in boot, considered for use against Vampire Duke.
Used by Bjorn to temporarily seal the Vampire Duke's resistances and cause damage, then destroyed.
Raven discovers that Bjorn used the Tear of the Goddess against the Vampire Duke and learns its historical significance.

information.label: Discovery
information.notes: Retrieved from a broken fountain statue in the fortress courtyard after the party escaped the vampire; Bjorn stashes it in his boot groove. The game message confirms acquisition: 'Character has acquired [Tear of the Goddess].'

information.label: Properties

attributes.effect: Heals ally or damages Evil Attribute monsters

information.notes: Hidden in Bjorn's boot for emergency use against the Vampire Duke.

information.label: Usage
information.notes: Bjorn shoved the Tear of the Goddess into the vampire's mouth and forced its jaw shut. The item was destroyed, triggering Grace which temporarily sealed the vampire's resistances and caused great internal damage.

name: Vampire Guardian Essence
short_description: The specific Guardian's Essence dropped by the Vampire Duke Cambormir in the Bloodstained Fortress. Absorbed by Bjorn Yandel. Stats: Natural Regeneration +45, Strength +15, Agility +15, Magic Resistance +30, Physical Resistance +12, Soul Power +21, Mana Sensitivity +30, Dark Resistance +12, Sunlight Resistance -12. Skills: (P) Source of Darkness, (A) Sacrificial Mark, (A) Immortal One, (A) Blood Master.

chapter_mentions.context: Raven reveals the exact stats and four skills of the Vampire Guardian Essence to Bjorn after examination.

name: Chronicles of the Rift III
short_description: An old book containing knowledge about Rifts, including the Bloodstained Fortress. It details hidden passages, how to obtain the Necronomicon from the Demon Worship Hall, and mentions the Tear of the Goddess and Golden Mask with their acquisition methods partially erased by weathering. Raven owns it and uses it to research the Tear of the Goddess.

chapter_mentions.context: Raven reads the book to investigate the Tear of the Goddess, confirming its origin and effect.

name: Hans
short_description: An Explorer Bjorn meets at an inn after Ainar leaves. He advises Bjorn to use the Guild to find new party members, noting that recording orbs have become mandatory to prevent betrayal. He treats Bjorn to drinks and offers friendly advice.

chapter_mentions.context: Approaches Bjorn at the inn and suggests using the Guild to find companions, convincing Bjorn to give it a try.

name: Cardinal Androne
short_description: A historical figure who used all three Tears of the Goddess 150 years ago during the Dimensional Collapse to save the Pope, after which the artifacts vanished.

chapter_mentions.context: Mentioned in Raven's book as the user of the Tear of the Goddess during the Dimensional Collapse.

name: Dimensional Collapse
short_description: A historical event that occurred 150 years ago, during which Cardinal Androne used the Tears of the Goddess to save the Pope. Referenced in the Chronicles of the Rift III.

chapter_mentions.context: Mentioned as the time when Cardinal Androne used the Tear of the Goddess.

name: Terves
short_description: A polite and efficient guild administrator who processed Bjorn's request for companions at the Explorer's Guild near the Mage Tower.

chapter_mentions.context: Handles Bjorn's registration for finding party members; his overly polite and perfect mannerisms strike Bjorn as strange.

name: Erwen Fornachi di Tersia

aliases.name: Erwen Witember di Tersia
aliases.context: Alternative full name revealed in chapter 28

short_description: A 20-year-old Fairy explorer, younger sister of Daria Witember di Tersia. After returning from a Labyrinth expedition with her sister, she searched for Bjorn at his inn only to be told he was dead; she resolved to become stronger. She has been training intensely, summoning spirits constantly, and now hears the voice of the fire, developing a desperate and malicious determination.

chapter_mentions.context: Encountered by Bjorn while injured; initially feared him but accepted his offer of help.
Introduced by full name, becomes Bjorn's Night Companion after swearing a mutual oath.
Reveals her pursuers are the Crystal Alliance, demonstrates bow and fire spirit magic, kills Serdin.
Kills Serdin and agrees to accompany Bjorn to the Second Floor with a 9:1 split.
Confidently detects and avoids traps, scouts ahead, and shoots a goblin with precision, proving her value as a partner.
Meets Bjorn at Paidenfuss tavern, refuses his party offer to join her sister Tersia, but later invites him to her room to demonstrate her Supernatural Ability.
Accompanies Bjorn to sell loot, displays greed for money, and they part ways at the Central Plaza.
Successfully contracted a Wind Spirit and visited Bjorn in the morning to show off.
Under training by her sister Tersia on the First Floor, growing stronger and eager to rejoin Bjorn.
Returns from a Labyrinth expedition only to find Bjorn missing and presumed dead; resolves to become stronger.
Shown training relentlessly at the inn, hearing the voice of the fire and becoming more desperate and driven, which concerns her sister Daria.

information.label: Character Development
information.notes: After being told Bjorn is dead, she squatted in front of the inn for two days, then decided to become stronger.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Pointy-eared, tiny frame.

information.label: Personality
information.notes: Initially extremely timid and wary, but after forming an oath with Bjorn, shows curiosity and a more relaxed side.

information.label: Background
information.notes: Has a younger sibling in the city. Belongs to the Fornachi di Tersia tribe.

information.label: Bow Proficiency
information.notes: Skillful with a bow; shot Serdin through the forehead without hesitation when Bjorn gave the signal.

information.label: Fire Spirit Magic
information.notes: Can summon a flame spirit to provide light and likely for combat.

information.label: Combat Resolve
information.notes: Despite initial hesitation, she killed a human pursuer decisively when ordered by Bjorn, showing she can take lethal action when necessary.

information.label: Archery Skill
information.notes: Shoots Goblins at point-blank range with precision; aims for the space between the eyes; recovers arrows after use.

information.label: Spirit Magic
information.notes: Summons a fist-sized flame that can last up to ten hours for illumination.

information.label: Determination
information.notes: Expresses eagerness to earn money for her younger brother; agrees to the 9:1 split without protest.

information.label: Trap Detection
information.notes: Demonstrates excellent trap detection ability, spotting a hidden trap under leaves from a distance in the darkness of the Goblin Forest. She is also able to scout silently and determine enemy positions.

information.label: Wind Spirit Contract
information.notes: Successfully contracted a Wind Spirit using a Spirit Stone after about 10 tries, spending a fortune. The Wind Spirit allows her to alter arrow trajectory or shoot faster.

name: Daria Witember di Tersia
short_description: A silver-haired fifth-year Fairy Explorer, elder sister of Erwen. She currently trains her sister on the First Floor, preparing for the Rift. Protective of her sister, she decides to withhold news about the Barbarian of Freedom to keep Erwen focused.

chapter_mentions.context: Arrives at Paidenfuss tavern to retrieve Erwen, pays Bjorn for his share, and takes her sister into her party.
Training her younger sister Erwen on the First Floor, preparing for the impending Rift opening.
Observed overhearing a conversation about the Barbarian of Freedom and deciding not to inform her sister, showing her protective nature.

information.label: Protective and Cautious
information.notes: After overhearing rumors about the Barbarian of Freedom, Daria decided not to tell her sister Erwen, believing it would be best for her to stay focused on training.

name: Crystal Alliance
short_description: An Explorer Clan that built a wide communication network using Message Stones, recently dismantled due to corruption and involvement in the false accusation against Bjorn Yandel. Their executive Hartz Young attacked Erwen, and she severed his genitals in self-defense. They have been pursuing her since, using Message Stones to coordinate. Bjorn Yandel demanded that the Explorer's Guild investigate the Crystal Alliance for their role in the false testimony of Hartz Young.

chapter_mentions.context: Revealed to be the group hunting Erwen after she severed an executive's genitals; they use Message Stones to coordinate tracking.
Hartz Young raises reward for Erwen to 20,000 Stone; they use Message Stones to coordinate.
Bjorn Yandel demands an investigation into the Crystal Alliance for their involvement in the false testimony.
