Heard to have been dismantled due to corruption and ties to the false accusation against Bjorn, providing a topic of conversation among explorers at the tavern.

information.label: Modus Operandi
information.notes: Uses coordinated pursuit via Message Stones; operatives relay location information to converge on targets.

information.label: Operations
information.notes: Members use Message Stones for short-range communication; they coordinate pursuit of Erwen across the First Floor.

information.label: Leadership
information.notes: Executive Hartz Young offers a reward of 20,000 Stone and a claim on Erwen to motivate members.

name: Golden Mask
short_description: A unique helmet that allows the wearer to customize their facial appearance for thirty days, with a limited number of uses (max five times); obtainable after defeating the boss of the Bloodstained Fortress and satisfying special conditions. It is a consumable item worth about 500,000 Stone on the Exchange.

chapter_mentions.context: Listed in the book alongside the Tear of the Goddess, but details are illegible.

information.label: Effect

attributes.effect: Allows wearer to customize facial appearance for 30 days

information.notes: Consumable item that disappears after use limit is reached.

name: Tarikan, son of Riyen
short_description: An Evil Spirit (player reincarnated) from another world, summoned into the body of a Barbarian named Tarikan son of Riyen a month before Bjorn. He survived his Coming of Age Ceremony by being the last called, avoiding detection. However, he failed to enter the Labyrinth due to fear, sold his starting weapon for 50,000 Stone, and has been struggling to survive for three months, homeless and starving. He approached Bjorn for help; Bjorn gave him 150,000 Stone and told him never to return.

chapter_mentions.context: Discovered by Bjorn in a street corner, a destitute Barbarian without a weapon; Bjorn quickly identifies him as a newly arrived Evil Spirit.
Bjorn encounters Tarikan, gives him 150,000 Stone, and warns him to never seek him again.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Leaning against an alley wall, looking utterly miserable and beggarly.

information.label: Behavioral Traits
information.notes: Uses formal speech (e.g., 'I… I am Ta-Tarikan, son of Riyen!'), is easily startled, and sold his weapon due to poverty. Bjorn suspects he is an Evil Spirit (reincarnated being from another world) due to lacking the typical Barbarian pride and weapon.

information.label: Background and Struggles
information.notes: Summoned a month before Bjorn, survived the Coming of Age Ceremony by being the last called. Sold his starting weapon for 50,000 Stone and has been homeless and starving for three months, unable to adapt to the Barbarian lifestyle.

name: Bjorn Yandel

aliases.name: Bjorn
aliases.context: Short form of Bjorn Yandel, his given name.

aliases.name: Bjorn, son of Yandel, the greatest warrior
aliases.context: Title given by fellow Barbarians after his successful return from the Second Floor

aliases.name: Barbarian of Freedom
aliases.context: Title given by rumormongers for his actions against the Guild, exaggerating his deeds.

short_description: Main protagonist, a Barbarian Explorer. Son of Yandel. After a month of training, entered the Labyrinth using dimensional instability exploit. Currently Level 3 with stats: Physical 155, Mental 90, Supernatural Ability 115. Possesses Immortal Engraving Stage 1. Recently absorbed Corpse Golem Essence (Rank 7) and Vampire Guardian Essence (Rank 5). The Vampire Guardian Essence grants four skills: (P) Source of Darkness — resists death as long as heart is intact; (A) Sacrificial Mark — temporarily increases physical abilities based on marked living creatures; (A) Immortal One — greatly increases regeneration in proportion to lost health; (A) Blood Master — absorbs one target skill upon direct vampirism. Survived the Vampire Duke fight through sheer will and potion usage. After being unjustly sentenced to death by the Explorer's Guild, he orchestrates a prison break, takes hostages, and forces a public trial. He is ultimately cleared, promoted to Rank 7, and receives 5 million Stones in compensation. Later visits the Mage Tower, negotiates with Raven's master Deian, and acquires the Frost Spirit Ring as additional compensation. In Chapter 43, he reflects on his personality changes. In Chapter 47, he purchased high-tier gear: a Lithium Alloy Breastplate (1,910,000 Stone), a Lithium Alloy battle Shield (2,100,000 Stone), and an Expansion Bag (2,500,000 Stone), raising his Item Level to 683 and Overall Combat Index to 527.75. He then met with Hikurod Murad to discuss joining a team.

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as the protagonist, a Barbarian explorer who recently died and revived, now navigating the Labyrinth.
Kills his Night Companion Hans after the latter attempts to murder him for his heart, and gains a permanent Mental Power increase.
Continues exploring the First Floor, grows exhausted from lack of sleep, and decides to find a non-human Night Companion; encounters a wounded Fairy.
Forces Erwen to reveal her past and then proposes a temporary companion oath to deal with the Crystal Alliance.
Leads Erwen to the Second Floor, hunting Ghouls and evading the Crystal Alliance.
Returns to Rafdonia after a week in the Labyrinth, earns over 180,000 Stone, and is celebrated by Barbarians as the greatest warrior; he also buys new clothes and reflects on his desire to go home.
Wakes up after sleeping over 30 hours, meets Erwen at Paidenfuss tavern, negotiates with Tersia, and makes an oath not to sleep with Erwen.
Explores Rafdonia, sells loot, and formulates a plan to reach the Sixth Floor for financial security.
Proposes a team with Ainar and requests the Immortal Engraving from the Tribe Chief.
Received the Immortal Engraving Stage 1, bought new equipment, and established a daily routine of training and studying.
Leveled up to Level 2 by opening the First Floor portal first, then used dimensional instability to skip directly to the Second Floor.
Bjorn leads Ainar through the Land of the Dead, uses Barbarian Candle Mode, and fights the Death Fiend, gaining EXP from its defeat.
Survived a fatal neck wound thanks to Immortal Engraving, killing Darvan and freeing Ainar before collapsing.
Forced to swear an Oath to survive after being paralyzed by the Raider; vows to become stronger.
Decides to become proactive and forcibly opens a Rift using the Monument to the Great Sage.
Enters the Bloodstained Fortress Rift with Ainar and other explorers, assesses the team dynamics, and decides to go along with Raven's leadership.
Leads the party to defeat the Vampire Duke, using desperate tactics including consuming the Corpse Golem Essence and repeatedly casting Flesh Explosion to destroy the vampire's heart.

Wakes up after three days of unconsciousness, confirms his health, and proceeds to exchange Magic Stones at the Exchange Office for Ninth-Rank Explorers in Rafdonia, where he is suddenly arrested by guards.
Imprisoned and sentenced to death by the Explorer's Guild for alleged raiding; plans to escape and exact revenge.
Escapes from imprisonment, leads a prison riot, takes the Branch Chief and Regional Chief's daughter hostage to force a public trial of his innocence.
Continues his escape by holding the Branch Chief and Regional Chief's daughter hostage, forcing them to summon key witnesses and a Priest to prove his innocence.
Successfully proves his innocence and secures compensation, including rank promotion to Rank 7 and 5 million Stones.
Visits the Mage Tower, negotiates with Raven and her master Deian, and accepts the Frost Spirit Ring as compensation for the Corpse Golem Essence.
Reflects on his increasingly aggressive instincts and the fusion of his original personality with the Barbarian body's instincts.
Visits Raven's lab to review his Essence, learns the precise stats and skills of his Vampire Guardian Essence, and considers solo play or finding new party members.
Purchased high-tier gear including Lithium Alloy armor and shield and an expansion bag, then met with Hikurod Murad to discuss joining his team.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Wields a Shield; covered in blood from Goblin fights.

information.label: Skills
information.notes: Uses improvised skills 'Smash' (attack with shield, 0 MP) and 'Final Shield Attack' (a finishing shield blow).

information.label: Cautious Nature
information.notes: Refuses to fully trust a Night Companion despite forming a partnership; pretends to doze off to stay alert.

information.label: Mental Power
information.notes: Permanently increased by 1 after killing Hans in self-defense.

information.label: Deceptive Nature
information.notes: Uses the Barbarian stereotype of being simple and foolish to hide his true intentions. Feigned snoring to test Hans's trustworthiness.

information.label: Stats
information.notes: After absorbing the Corpse Golem and Vampire Guardian essences, Bjorn's stats increased dramatically. His Mental Power permanently increased by +10 due to an achievement for surviving within the countdown.

information.label: Equipment
information.notes: Equipped leather boots, two-handed hammer, shoulder guards, backpack. Also carries water flask, compass, pocket watch, and potion.

information.label: Combat Efficiency
information.notes: Easily defeats Ghouls with Hammer Smash; coordinates ranged and melee attacks with Erwen.

information.label: Leadership
information.notes: Decides to head to Second Floor with Erwen, setting a 9:1 split of spoils.

information.label: Resourcefulness
information.notes: Uses the Message Stone from Serdin to monitor pursuers; navigates using darkness as a guide to the upper floor.

information.label: Oath
information.notes: Swore on his honor as a Warrior not to sleep with Erwen, as demanded by Tersia.

information.label: Companion Acquisition
information.notes: After sparring with Ainar, Bjorn offers her a team with an 8:2 split in his favor, and she accepts. He also bears the cost of the team.

information.label: Goal: Spirit Engraving
information.notes: Bjorn decides to use his 1.4 million Stone to request the Immortal Engraving from the Tribe Chief at the Holy Land, a costly Barbarian growth method.

information.label: Immortal Engraving Stage 1
information.notes: Bjorn underwent the Immortal Engraving, which infused soul power into his body. The process was painful and took until dark. He felt a strange power stirring.

information.label: Equipment Upgrades
information.notes: Bought a one-handed mace, a half armor (bulletproof vest style), and a custom helmet. The helmet required custom fitting and took 3 days.

information.label: Daily Routine
information.notes: Established a strict daily routine focused on study, combat training, and socializing with Erwen. Sparring with Barbarians improved his reflexes and dynamic vision.

information.label: Level Up (Level 2)
information.notes: Gained level by being the first to open the First Floor portal, earning 2 EXP.

information.label: Barbarian Candle Mode
information.notes: Improvised technique of attaching a torch to his helmet to provide hands-free light, commonly used by other Explorers.

information.label: Battle Cry
information.notes: Bjorn shouts 'Behel' as a Barbarian war cry during combat, referencing the ancestral spirit.

information.label: Combat Behavior
information.notes: Allowed himself to be stabbed in the neck to break Suppression, then threw the knife at Ainar to free her, demonstrating extreme willingness to use pain and injury to turn the tide.

information.label: Determination
information.notes: After the Raider encounter, Bjorn vows to become stronger, reflecting on his past and using his disgust as fuel. He recalls the advice 'Remember, you're nothing' to push himself.

information.label: Proactive Mindset
information.notes: Decides to stop merely reacting and start seeking out challenges proactively, stating 'Strike first, win.' Plans to enter Rifts to grow stronger.

information.label: Rift Opening Knowledge
information.notes: Knows how to forcibly open a Rift on the First Floor by offering Magic Stones of four types (Goblin, Gnome, Ghoul, Blade Wolf) and an Eighth-Rank Magic Stone at the Monument to the Great Sage.

information.label: Essences
information.notes: Bjorn consumed the Corpse Golem Essence during the battle, gaining Pain Resistance, Strength, Bone Density, etc. Later absorbed the Vampire Guardian Essence after defeating the Vampire Duke, gaining Natural Regeneration, Strength, Agility, Magic Resistance, and the passive Source of Darkness and Immortal One.

information.label: Passive Skills

attributes.source_of_darkness: Prevents death until heart is destroyed
attributes.immortal_one: Greatly increases regeneration in proportion to lost health

information.notes: Both passives obtained from the Vampire Guardian Essence. Source of Darkness ensures Bjorn cannot die as long as his heart remains intact. Immortal One boosts regeneration when health is low.

information.label: Imprisonment
information.notes: Arrested by the Explorer's Guild on suspicion of raiding, held in an interrogation room in the Guild basement, sentenced to death after a corrupt investigation. Plans to escape and exact revenge.

information.label: Strategic Planning
information.notes: Planned the entire prison break and hostage situation in about 30 minutes, aiming to create a situation where the Guild cannot silence him.

information.label: Compensation
information.notes: Received after the Regional Chief conceded to demands.

information.label: Trial Outcome
information.notes: Declared innocent by the Mage's recording magic; the Guild's corruption was exposed.

information.label: Social Manipulator
information.notes: Engages in sycophantic conversation with Master Deian, praising him to gain favor, and later switches to serious negotiation. Demonstrates ability to read the room and adapt his behavior.

information.label: Personality Change
information.notes: Reflects on his increasingly aggressive instincts and the fusion of his original personality with the Barbarian body's instincts, feeling that he has become more aggressive and simple-minded like the Barbarians, a change noticed since waking up in this body.

name: Lithium Alloy Breastplate
short_description: A Tier 2 metal breastplate made of lithium alloy, white in color, extremely light and hard. Purchased by Bjorn for 1,910,000 Stone to maximize protection of his heart, synergizing with the Source of Darkness passive.

chapter_mentions.context: Purchased to maximize protection of his heart, synergizing with Source of Darkness.

name: Lithium Alloy battle Shield
short_description: A large shield made of lithium alloy, purchased for 2,100,000 Stone. Used by Bjorn to become a human wall and protect companions.

chapter_mentions.context: Bought to become a human wall for team support.

name: Expansion Bag
short_description: An Eighth Rank storage backpack with enchantments: Auto-Sort, Individual Withdrawal, Lighten (Low). Purchased for 2,500,000 Stone to greatly increase inventory capacity and convenience.

chapter_mentions.context: Acquired to greatly increase inventory capacity and convenience.

name: Alminus Central Exchange
short_description: The exchange in the commercial city of Commelby, Zone 2, where items can be searched and purchased via consignment sale. Used by Bjorn to buy gear by submitting search criteria and paying a fee per item.

chapter_mentions.context: First visited to purchase the Expansion Bag and later the armor and shield.

name: Vampire (Guardian) Essence
short_description: Guardian-type essence obtained from the Vampire Duke Cambormir, granting four skills: Source of Darkness (passive), Sacrificial Mark, Immortal One, and Blood Master. These skills affect combat strategy and gear choices.

chapter_mentions.context: Referenced as the source of Bjorn's Source of Darkness passive, influencing his equipment choices.

name: Guardian's Wristguard
short_description: A Numbers Item (No. 3112) obtained by Hikurod from the Bloodstained Fortress. Worth approximately 50 million Stone. Only obtainable on floors 4+.

chapter_mentions.context: Revealed by Hikurod as the Numbers Item he obtained from the Bloodstained Fortress, impressing Bjorn.

information.label: Value
information.notes: Only obtainable from Rifts on the Fourth Floor or higher.

name: House of Baron Martoan
short_description: A noble house in Rafdonia to which Dwalki claims distant relation via his aunt's husband.

chapter_mentions.context: Mentioned by Dwalki as the baronial house he is distantly related to.

name: Red Beast Tribe
short_description: One of the five great tribes, Misha Karlstein's tribe.

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as the tribe of Beastkin explorer Misha Karlstein.

information.label: Background
information.notes: One of the five great beast tribes. Misha Karlstein is the princess of this tribe.

name: Hikurod Murad

aliases.name: Murad
aliases.context: Common short form used by Bjorn and others.

short_description: A Dwarf Explorer and team leader. Previously partnered with Bjorn in the Bloodstained Fortress, obtaining the Guardian's Wristguard worth ~50 million Stone. Promoted to Sixth Rank. In Chapter 49, he shows pragmatic and supportive leadership, taking care of Dwalki and managing team dynamics.

chapter_mentions.context: Joins Bjorn and Ainar in the Bloodstained Fortress Rift as a random explorer.
Reintroduced as a third-year Explorer in the Bloodstained Fortress Rift.
Negotiated loot distribution alongside Raven and Bjorn, securing the Numbers Item for himself.
The Dwarf warrior is wounded in the eye by the Corpse Golem's attack, incapacitating him and forcing Bjorn to take the lead.
Froze and was wounded by the Corpse Golem's attack, but later recovered and supported the party.
Engages the Vampire Duke but is incapacitated, forcing Bjorn and Ainar to fight alone.
Used Lightning Discharge to stun the Vampire Duke, creating an opening for Bjorn's attack.
Hikurod Murad is recruiting a team for a Third Floor expedition, and Bjorn considers applying.
Contacted Bjorn via letter and met him at a tavern to discuss joining his expedition team.
Bjorn meets Hikurod to discuss team formation; Hikurod reveals his valuable Numbers Item and gets promoted to Sixth Rank.
Serves as team leader, now comfortable with Dwalki and showing supportive leadership.

information.label: Fighting Style
information.notes: Uses a hammer and methodically crushes enemies from the bottom up, as seen with the Gargoyle Statue.

information.label: Personality
information.notes: Friendly at first but becomes confrontational over money; negotiates hard for the Numbers Item.

information.label: Injury
information.notes: Incapacitated by a piece of flesh from the Corpse Golem's attack that hit his eye through his helmet's slit, causing him to be temporarily blinded in that eye.

information.label: Vulnerability to Shock
information.notes: Froze in fear upon seeing the Corpse Golem, resulting in a wound to his eye from acidic fluid. Later recovered and continued fighting.

information.label: Essence Abilities
information.notes: Used these abilities to hold the line against the Vampire Duke before being overwhelmed.

information.label: Combat Role
information.notes: Murad used the combo of 'Lightning' and 'Discharge' to stun the Vampire Duke, allowing Bjorn to shove the Tear of the Goddess into its mouth. He was later incapacitated by Pain Sharing.

information.label: Promotion
information.notes: After Bjorn's suggestion, Hikurod submitted a rank review application and was promoted to Sixth Rank within six days.

information.label: Possesses Numbers Item
information.notes: Has the Guardian's Wristguard (No. 3112) from the Bloodstained Fortress, valued at 50 million Stone.

information.label: Leadership Style
information.notes: Pragmatic and supportive, takes care of Dwalki despite his eccentricities, respects team members' opinions and learns to be a good team leader.

name: Riol Worb Dwalki

aliases.name: Dwalki
aliases.context: Mage applying to the team, uses this short form.

short_description: An Eighth Rank Mage who officially joins Bjorn and Hikurod's team. Claims recognition from the Rafdonia Royal Family and ties to the House of Baron Martoan. In reality, he is a con artist-like figure but a genuine mage, skilled in lifestyle magic, curses, and Ice Magic. He is inexperienced in the Labyrinth and has a bluffing personality.

chapter_mentions.context: Applies to join Bjorn and Hikurod's team, boasting of his noble connections and official Mage recognition.
Joins the team as the first mage companion, immediately casting a blinding spell upon entering the Labyrinth.

information.label: Speech Pattern
information.notes: Speaks in a proud and convoluted manner, often confusing others. Introduces himself with elaborate titles.

information.label: Personality
information.notes: Full of bluff and bluster, reeks of a con artist but is genuine and follows orders well.

information.label: Magic Abilities
information.notes: Casts a bright Light spell to illuminate the dark, though wasteful of mana.

name: Misha Karlstein
short_description: Beastkin Explorer, princess of the Red Beast Tribe, speaks with a slur; member of Bjorn's team.

chapter_mentions.context: Introduced as a Beastkin explorer of the Red Beast Tribe, joining the team as a warrior.

information.label: Appearance
information.notes: Cold atmosphere created by her appearance.

information.label: Speech Pattern
information.notes: Has a habit of slurring only the ends of her words, claiming it's due to Beastkin anatomy.

information.label: Background
information.notes: Princess of the Red Beast Tribe, one of the five great tribes. The clan chief is her father.

name: Brown Rotmiller
short_description: Human Seventh Rank Scout with eight years of exploration experience; the most experienced member of the team.

chapter_mentions.context: Joins the team as a veteran Scout, taking charge of navigation and leading the party through the First Floor.

information.label: Personality
information.notes: Serious and inflexible, likely developed for survival. He is the only normal person on the team.

information.label: Experience
information.notes: Survived in the Labyrinth for eight years despite low rank, indicating reliance on other strengths.

name: Crystal Cave
short_description: The first floor of the Labyrinth, a cave filled with crystals. Also a specific cave area within the First Floor where Tersia trained Erwen.

chapter_mentions.context: Tersia and Erwen are training in the Crystal Cave on the First Floor.
The party enters the Crystal Cave, the first floor of the Labyrinth, at the start of their expedition.

name: Dimensional Instability Phenomenon
short_description: A method used by experienced explorers to unconditionally acquire portal opening experience points by exploiting dimensional instability, requiring security through promises or oaths.

chapter_mentions.context: Bjorn considers using this method to gain portal opening experience points but decides to wait for a trustworthy team.

name: Beast Den
short_description: The second floor of the Labyrinth, a den of beasts.

chapter_mentions.context: The party enters the Beast Den, the second floor, after passing through the portal on the first floor.

name: Baron Martoan
short_description: A noble house that Dwalki claims relation to via his aunt's husband's brother.

chapter_mentions.context: Dwalki claims ties to this barony to boast about his status.
